certificate: gamma-frontier certificate: every relevant Gini interval was either closed with exact pricing or fathomed by a valid interval lower bound above the verified incumbent objective
notes: parsed Hybrid GA text format; legacy weights with max 10 scaled by 0.1 to match Hybrid GA parser; distances rebuilt from points at speed factor 1.5

Gamma-frontier diagnostic covers Gini intervals with fixed-Gini interval branch-price trees. It reports optimal only if every relevant interval is closed or bound-fathomed and the aggregated lower bound reaches the incumbent. Each interval also uses the valid trivial bound objective>=G>=interval_floor and a final-inventory pickup/route/Gini relaxation bound when it solves.

BPC thread policy: requested_threads=1, bpc_workers=8, pricing_threads=1, parallel_frontier=true, parallel_nodes=false, hardware_concurrency=20; restricted-master CPLEX and inventory-bound CPLEX calls use one internal thread each
accepted external JSON incumbent for frontier cutoff only: objective=0.463263, G=0.345161, P=0.787344
external incumbent JSON is used only as a feasible route warm start/cutoff; no lower-bound certificate is inherited from results\paper_tailored_v10_m2_average.json
